column2: Eco‑corridor grammars & invariant control for water machines
column6: Completes normalized‑riskrxr_xrxand Lyapunov‑residualVtV_tVtspines with Rust/ALN contracts enforcing “no corridor, no deployment” and “violation → derate/stop” across all cyboquatic nodes.

column2: Cyboquatic MAR modules for desert cities (Phoenix‑class)
column6: Refines SAT corridors for PFAS, pharmaceuticals, nutrients, temperature, and fouling in 30 m × 4 m recharge vaults so future modules can restore on the order of 10 m groundwater over decades under hard safety limits.

column2: Cyboquatic ecotechnology for bioremediation & habitat repair
column6: Builds supervisory controllers that route flows through wetlands, biofilms, and biochar under strict nutrient/DO/temperature/CEC/shear corridors, increasing pollutant removal without additional heavy concrete.

Designs soft, partially biodegradable canal/wetland robots with tuned lifetimes, non‑toxic breakdown products, and ultra‑low‑power sensing to reduce disturbance and microplastics while extending monitoring reach.

column2: Circular‑materials & refurbishment loops for cyboquatic hardware
column6: Formalizes design‑for‑disassembly, refurbishment gates, and LCA corridors so pumps, turbines, and sensor racks must demonstrate up to ~80% embedded‑energy savings versus new builds before deployment.
